nodes.name: Tulu 3 8B preference mix with Qwen3 think responses

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: The released SmolLM3-3B instruct/reasoning checkpoint is the post-trained model whose HF model tree lists SmolLM3-3B-Base as its base; the blog describes SFT, APO, and final merging after the base/mid-training stages.

evidence.explanation: The model card directly identifies SmolLM3-3B-Base as the base model for the released SmolLM3-3B checkpoint.

evidence.excerpt: Following the reasoning mid-training stage... we proceed with Supervised Finetuning (SFT)
evidence.explanation: The blog describes the subsequent post-training stages that transform the base/mid-training checkpoint into the released instruct/reasoning model.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: SmolLM3-3B-Base was trained on an 11.2T-token three-stage pretraining curriculum mixing web, code, math, and reasoning data with changing ratios across stable and decay phases.

evidence.location: Data mixture and training stages, lines 170-187
evidence.excerpt: we train SmolLM3 on 11.2T tokens using a three-stage training strategy that mixes web, math, and code data
evidence.explanation: Documents the aggregate three-stage pretraining mixture used to train SmolLM3 before post-training.

evidence.excerpt: SmolLM3-3B-Base is the base model after pretraining
evidence.explanation: Connects the pretraining recipe to the released base checkpoint.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: After main pretraining, the base lineage received 100B additional long-context tokens in two 50B-token stages, first 4k→32k then 32k→64k, upsampling math, code, and reasoning data.

evidence.excerpt: we trained SmolLM3 on an additional 100B tokens to extend its context length
evidence.explanation: Documents the long-context extension data as a further training stage in the SmolLM3 base lineage.

evidence.excerpt: Long context: Trained on 64k context and suppots up to 128k tokens using YARN extrapolation
evidence.explanation: The base card states the released base has the long-context training outcome.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: FineWeb-Edu was part of the SmolLM3 pretraining web mixture, including Stage 1 web data and ablation-scale runs.

evidence.explanation: The Hugging Face collection is described as datasets used in SmolLM3 pretraining and lists FineWeb-Edu.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: DCLM Baseline 1.0 was included in the SmolLM3 pretraining web mixture.

evidence.explanation: The pretraining-datasets collection lists DCLM Baseline 1.0 as one of the datasets used in SmolLM3 pretraining.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: FineWeb2-HQ supplied multilingual web data for SmolLM3 pretraining; the blog says FineWeb2-HQ was part of Stage 1 web data and configs use it for many languages.

evidence.explanation: The SmolLM3 pretraining collection lists FineWeb2-HQ as a pretraining dataset.

evidence.location: Data mixture and training stages, lines 174-178
evidence.excerpt: Web: 85% (12% multilingual) - FineWeb-Edu, DCLM, FineWeb2 and FineWeb2-HQ
evidence.explanation: The blog names FineWeb2-HQ in the Stage 1 web mixture.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: FineWeb2 supplied multilingual web data for SmolLM3 pretraining, including Hindi, Thai, and Korean language slices per configs.

evidence.explanation: The SmolLM3 pretraining collection lists FineWeb2 as a dataset used for pretraining.

evidence.location: Data mixture and training stages, lines 174-178
evidence.explanation: The blog names FineWeb2 in the Stage 1 web mixture.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: FineMath and InfiWebMath subsets in HuggingFaceTB/finemath were used as SmolLM3 pretraining math data: FineMath3+/InfiWebMath3+ in Stage 1 and FineMath4+/InfiWebMath4+ from Stage 2 onward.

evidence.explanation: The collection lists HuggingFaceTB/finemath as a SmolLM3 pretraining dataset.

evidence.location: Data mixture and training stages, lines 178-182
evidence.explanation: The blog names FineMath and InfiWebMath subsets as pretraining math data.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: The Stack v2 supplied SmolLM3 code pretraining data across 16 programming languages, with related StarCoder2 pull-request/code material in the code mixture.

evidence.explanation: The pretraining collection lists The Stack v2 among SmolLM3 pretraining datasets.

evidence.location: Data mixture and training stages, lines 176-178
evidence.excerpt: Code: 12% - The Stack v2 (16 programming languages)
evidence.explanation: The blog names The Stack v2 as the Stage 1 code source.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: Issues, Kaggle, and notebook data were part of SmolLM3 code pretraining, corresponding to GitHub issues, Kaggle notebooks, and Jupyter notebook sources.

evidence.explanation: The SmolLM3 pretraining collection lists the HuggingFaceTB issues/kaggle/notebooks dataset.

evidence.location: Data mixture and training stages, lines 176-178
evidence.excerpt: Jupyter and Kaggle notebooks, GitHub issues
evidence.explanation: The blog names the notebook and issue sources in the code pretraining mixture.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: StackExchange data was included in the SmolLM3 pretraining mixture, including the StackExchange 2025 Markdown dataset listed in the SmolLM3 pretraining collection.

evidence.explanation: The collection lists StackExchange 2025 Markdown as part of SmolLM3 pretraining.

evidence.location: Data mixture and training stages, lines 176-178
evidence.explanation: The blog names StackExchange in the code/data mixture.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: MegaMath was introduced in SmolLM3 Stage 2 math pretraining, including Qwen Q&A, Pro synthetic rewrites, and text-code interleaved blocks.

evidence.explanation: The SmolLM3 pretraining collection lists MegaMath under Stage 2 new datasets.

evidence.location: Data mixture and training stages, lines 179-182
evidence.excerpt: MegaMath (including Qwen Q&A, Pro synthetic rewrites, and text-code interleaved blocks)
evidence.explanation: The blog states MegaMath entered the Stage 2 math mixture and names its subcomponents.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: Stack-Edu was added as higher-quality code data in SmolLM3 Stage 2 and remained in later code upsampling.

evidence.explanation: The pretraining collection lists Stack-Edu as a Stage 2 new dataset.

evidence.location: Data mixture and training stages, lines 179-182
evidence.explanation: The blog explicitly says Stack-Edu was added in Stage 2 code pretraining.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: SmolLM Corpus was included among SmolLM3 Stage 3 decay-phase pretraining datasets, covering internal corpus components such as Cosmopedia2 and multilingual wiki slices in configs.

evidence.explanation: The collection lists HuggingFaceTB/smollm-corpus under Stage 3 new datasets for SmolLM3 pretraining.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: Dolmino Mix 1124 components, including pes2o, wiki, and stackexchange slices, were used in SmolLM3 pretraining configs and the collection lists the Dolmino mix for Stage 3.

evidence.explanation: The SmolLM3 pretraining collection lists AllenAI Dolmino Mix 1124.
The training config lists Dolmino-style subcorpora such as pes2o, wiki, and stackexchange as dataset paths.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: OpenMathReasoning was introduced as reasoning/math data during the Stage 3 decay phase of SmolLM3 pretraining.

evidence.explanation: The collection lists OpenMathReasoning under Stage 3 new datasets for SmolLM3 pretraining.

evidence.location: Data mixture and training stages, lines 183-186
evidence.excerpt: introducing instruction and reasoning datasets such as OpenMathReasoning
evidence.explanation: The blog names OpenMathReasoning as a Stage 3 reasoning/math pretraining dataset.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: OpenCodeReasoning was included in the Stage 3 decay-phase SmolLM3 pretraining dataset collection as reasoning/code data.

evidence.explanation: The collection lists OpenCodeReasoning under Stage 3 new datasets used for SmolLM3 pretraining.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b-base
edges.description: Natural Reasoning was included among the Stage 3 decay-phase datasets used in SmolLM3 pretraining.

evidence.explanation: The SmolLM3 pretraining collection lists Facebook Natural Reasoning under Stage 3 new datasets.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: SmolTalk2 is the released aggregate post-training dataset for SmolLM3-3B, containing Mid, SFT, and Preference subsets corresponding to the three post-training phases.

evidence.excerpt: This dataset contains three subsets (Mid, SFT, Preference) that correspond to the three phases of Post-Training for SmolLM3-3B.
evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card directly states it corresponds to the SmolLM3-3B post-training phases.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: OpenThoughts3-1.2M was used in SmolLM3 reasoning mid-training and SFT: 1.14M Mid rows, 1.13M SFT think rows, and 435k no_think rows with reasoning traces removed.

evidence.excerpt: 35B tokens sourced from Open Thought’s OpenThoughts3-1.2M
evidence.explanation: The blog names OpenThoughts3-1.2M as a source for the reasoning mid-training data.
The SmolTalk2 card lists OpenThoughts3 in the Mid subset and again in SFT think/no_think subsets.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: SmolLM3's reasoning mid-training used a decontaminated subset of NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with R1 reasoning traces, 3.64M rows in SmolTalk2 Mid.

evidence.excerpt: a subset from NVIDIA’s Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with reasoning traces from R1
evidence.explanation: The blog names the NVIDIA dataset subset as a reasoning mid-training source.
The SmolTalk2 card quantifies the Llama-Nemotron portion of the Mid subset.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: SmolTalk supplied multiple SmolLM3 SFT slices: multilingual, everyday, systemchats, smol-magpie-ultra, explore-instruct-rewriting, smol-rewrite, and smol-summarize; several think variants were generated by Qwen3-32B from SmolTalk prompts.

evidence.excerpt: SmolTalk (multilingual-8languages): 244736 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in SmolTalk.
evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card lists SmolTalk-derived SFT subsets and states Qwen3-32B generated reasoning-mode variants from SmolTalk prompts.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: LongAlign-64k data was used in SmolLM3 SFT: a no_think lang_6 slice filtered up to 64k tokens and a Qwen3-32B-generated think slice from LongAlign-64k prompts.

evidence.excerpt: LongAlign-64k-context-lang-annotated: 7526 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in LongAlign-64k.
evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card lists LongAlign-64k-context-lang-annotated among think SFT data and describes the LongAlign no_think filtered slice.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: The SFT think mixture included 9,079 smolagents-toolcalling-traces examples with reasoning traces for tool-calling training.

evidence.location: SFT composition, lines 172-173 and stats line 230
evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card lists smolagents-toolcalling-traces in the SFT think datasets and gives its row count.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: The SFT think mixture included 28,217 Multi-Turn IF examples generated with a Multi-IF-style procedure using Tulu 3 Personas IF prompts, Qwen3-235B-A22B turns, and Qwen3-32B responses.

evidence.location: SFT composition, lines 174-175 and stats line 226
evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card lists Multi-Turn IF as an SFT think dataset and describes its generation procedure.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: The SmolLM3 SFT think mixture included the s1k-1.1 dataset, 835 examples in the released SmolTalk2 stats.

evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card lists s1k-1.1 as a think SFT dataset with its row count.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Aya prompts were used for a Qwen3-32B-generated SmolLM3 SFT think slice, with 15,222 Aya-derived rows.

evidence.excerpt: Aya: 15222 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in Aya.
evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card names Aya as a prompt source for a generated think SFT dataset.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Table-GPT was used in both no_think and Qwen3-32B-generated think SFT slices for SmolLM3.

evidence.excerpt: Table-GPT: 13201 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in Table-GPT.
evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card lists Table-GPT as an SFT source and also lists a Table-GPT no_think slice.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Tulu 3 SFT Personas IF contributed 29,970 no_think SFT examples and also served as the prompt source for generated Multi-Turn IF data.

evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card lists Tulu 3 SFT Personas IF as a no_think SFT dataset and says Multi-Turn IF prompts were sourced from Tulu 3 Personas IF.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Hermes-function-calling-v1 contributed 8,961 no_think tool/function-calling SFT examples.

evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card lists hermes-function-calling-v1 among no_think SFT datasets.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: xLAM traces contributed 59,962 no_think SFT tool-calling examples in the SmolTalk2 training statistics.

evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 stats table lists xlam-traces_no_think with 59,962 examples in the SFT mixture.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: OpenHermes-2.5 contributed 384,900 no_think SFT examples, with a 0.5 sampling weight in the SmolTalk2 stats.

evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card lists OpenHermes-2.5 as a no_think SFT dataset.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Mixture-of-Thoughts science contributed 86,110 no_think SFT examples after removing reasoning traces.

evidence.excerpt: Mixture of Thoughts (science): 86110 rows where we remove the reasoning trace.
evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card lists Mixture of Thoughts science as a no_think SFT source and describes trace removal.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: APO alignment used the Tulu 3 8B preference mixture for non-reasoning/no_think mode, with about 231k rows and a 0.5 sampling weight.

evidence.excerpt: using a combination of the Tulu3 preference dataset for non-reasoning mode
evidence.explanation: The blog states Tulu3 preference data was used for non-reasoning APO alignment.
The SmolTalk2 card quantifies the Tulu preference mixture used for SmolLM3 APO.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: APO also used a generated thinking-mode counterpart of the Tulu 3 8B preference mixture: 216k rows with Qwen3-32B chosen and Qwen3-0.6B rejected responses.

evidence.excerpt: Tulu 3 8B Preference Mixture (`think`): 216k rows where we generate the chosen responses with Qwen3-32B and the rejected responses with Qwen3-0.6B.
evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card describes the generated think preference data and its Qwen3 chosen/rejected sources.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Qwen3-32B generated SmolLM3 reasoning-mode SFT data from non-reasoning prompts and generated chosen responses for thinking-mode APO preference pairs; SmolTalk2 also names Qwen3-32B for SmolTalk, LongAlign, Aya, Table-GPT, and Multi-Turn IF responses.

evidence.excerpt: we generated synthetic data by prompting Qwen3-32B in reasoning mode
evidence.explanation: The blog states Qwen3-32B was prompted to generate reasoning-mode SFT data.

evidence.excerpt: We selected generations from Qwen3-32B as “chosen”
evidence.explanation: The blog states Qwen3-32B generations became chosen responses for APO preference training.

evidence.location: SFT and Preference Data, lines 167-179 and 198-200
evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card repeatedly names Qwen3-32B as the generator for specific SFT and preference subsets.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Qwen3-0.6B generated rejected responses for the thinking-mode APO preference pairs used to align SmolLM3-3B.

evidence.explanation: The blog states Qwen3-0.6B responses were selected as rejected completions for APO.
The SmolTalk2 card confirms Qwen3-0.6B generated rejected responses in the think preference dataset.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Qwen3-235B-A22B generated two verifiable turns for the Multi-Turn IF SFT data; Qwen3-32B then generated the final reasoning-mode responses.

evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card names Qwen3-235B-A22B as a generator in the Multi-Turn IF data-construction pipeline.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: DeepSeek-V3-0324 generated reasoning traces for the smolagents-toolcalling-traces SFT data used in SmolLM3 post-training.

evidence.excerpt: We generate tool calling data with reasoning traces using `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3-0324`.
evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card directly identifies DeepSeek-V3-0324 as the generator for the tool-calling reasoning traces.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: DeepSeek-R1 generated reasoning traces present in the Llama-Nemotron subset used for SmolLM3 reasoning mid-training.

evidence.excerpt: Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with reasoning traces from R1
evidence.explanation: The blog states the mid-training subset contained reasoning traces from R1.
The Llama-Nemotron dataset preview shows its generator field as DeepSeek-R1 for reasoning examples.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: OpenThoughts3-1.2M, which SmolLM3 used in mid-training and SFT, contains annotations generated by QwQ-32B.

evidence.explanation: Grounds that OpenThoughts3-1.2M entered SmolLM3 post-training.
The OpenThoughts3 card names QwQ-32B as the annotation model for that training dataset.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: The team explicitly followed Multi-IF's approach when constructing the new Multi-Turn IF SFT data, adapting it to Tulu 3 Personas IF prompts and Qwen generators.

evidence.excerpt: We follow Multi-IF's approach to generate multi turn data.
evidence.explanation: The SmolTalk2 card explicitly names Multi-IF as the borrowed data-generation approach.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: FineWeb-Edu supplied the 100B-token ablation training corpus used to validate SmolLM3 architecture modifications such as GQA and other changes before the final run.

evidence.excerpt: Our ablations on a 3B model trained with 100B tokens from FineWeb-Edu
evidence.explanation: The blog states FineWeb-Edu was the corpus used for 3B ablation models.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: RULER was used during long-context ablations and model-merging decisions, including tests of extra long-context data and recovery of long-context performance after APO.

evidence.location: Long Context extension, line 197; Model Merging, lines 237-245
evidence.excerpt: didn't further boost performance on RULER and HELMET benchmarks
evidence.explanation: The blog says RULER was used to assess long-context data ablations; later it says merging recovered the base model's RULER score.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: HELMET was used alongside RULER during ablations testing whether upsampling specific long-context data improved long-context performance.

evidence.excerpt: didn't further boost performance on RULER and HELMET benchmarks
evidence.explanation: The blog names HELMET as a benchmark used to judge the long-context data ablation.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: AIME 2025 was used to evaluate SmolLM3-3B in both no-thinking and extended-thinking instruction modes.

evidence.explanation: The SmolLM3-3B card reports AIME 2025 results for the instruction model, showing it was part of the evaluation suite.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: GSM-Plus was used as the math problem-solving evaluation benchmark for SmolLM3-3B instruction/no-thinking and reasoning modes.

evidence.explanation: The model card reports GSM-Plus scores for SmolLM3-3B, documenting its use as an evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: LiveCodeBench v4 was used to evaluate SmolLM3-3B competitive-programming performance in instruction and extended-thinking modes.

evidence.explanation: The card reports SmolLM3-3B performance on LiveCodeBench v4.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: GPQA Diamond was used as a graduate-level reasoning evaluation for SmolLM3-3B in both reported instruction modes.

evidence.explanation: The model card reports GPQA Diamond results for SmolLM3-3B.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: IFEval was used to evaluate SmolLM3-3B instruction following in no-thinking and extended-thinking modes.

evidence.explanation: The model card includes IFEval in the SmolLM3-3B instruction evaluation table.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: MixEval Hard was used as an alignment/chat evaluation benchmark for SmolLM3-3B.

evidence.explanation: The model card reports MixEval Hard results for SmolLM3-3B.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: BFCL was used to evaluate SmolLM3-3B tool-calling performance in both no-thinking and extended-thinking modes.

evidence.explanation: The model card includes BFCL in the tool-calling evaluation row.

edges.subject: huggingfacetb/smollm3-3b
edges.description: Global MMLU was used to evaluate SmolLM3-3B multilingual Q&A in the instruction/no-thinking and extended-thinking evaluation tables.

evidence.explanation: The model card reports Global MMLU scores for SmolLM3-3B.
